item.title: Romania to Hold Rates With Stagflation Risks Clouding Outlook
item.description: Romania is poised to keep interest rates at the highest level in the European Union as policymakers confront the dilemma of double-digit inflation and a deepening recession.

item.title: Copper Extends Retreat on Faster Inflation and Stronger Dollar
item.description: Copper extended its retreat from a record-high close as accelerating US inflation reduced the chance of rate cuts and a stronger dollar make the metal more expensive for many buyers.

item.title: Korea’s Kospi Races From 7,000 to Record 8,000 in Seven Sessions
item.description: South Korea’s benchmark stock index briefly breached the 8,000 mark for the first time on Friday, just seven sessions after it reached the 7,000 milestone.

item.title: Labor Unrest Threatens to Roil Australian LNG Export Projects
item.description: Australia’s liquefied natural gas sector is facing the prospect of industrial action at major export operations, adding another supply risk for Asian buyers already scrambling for cargoes after disruptions in the Middle East.

item.title: India’s Growing Rupee Defense Reassures Stock Bulls
item.description: Nation is also mulling a steep cut in the taxes paid by foreign investors on its bonds .

item.title: OpenAI May Raise More Money as Compute Crunch Deepens, CFO Says
item.description: OpenAI Chief Financial Officer Sarah Friar said the ChatGPT maker may raise more capital even after completing what she described as the largest private fundraising round ever, as the company races to secure computing power to meet surging AI demand.

item.title: UK Fund Seeks Removal of Two Executives at Tablet Maker Wacom
item.description: A UK investment fund is urging shareholders of pen tablet maker Wacom Co. to remove two top executives over the Japanese company’s weak performance and unsuccessful management moves.

item.title: Japan Producer Prices Jump by Most Since 2014, Backing BOJ Hike
item.description: Japan’s corporate goods prices surged in April by the most in 12 years, in another sign of how the war in Iran is boosting inflationary pressures and supporting the case for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates.

item.title: JPMorgan Hikes Taiex Bull-Case Target to 50,000 on AI Buildout
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised its targets for Taiwanese stocks for the second time in less than a month, recommending the market as “the most pure-play exposure to the global AI buildout.”

item.title: Trump Says China’s Xi Likes the Idea of Buying More Oil From US
item.description: President Donald Trump said his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping likes the idea of buying more US oil, as the leaders meet in Beijing.

item.title: India Hikes Fuel Prices for the First Time in Four Years
item.description: India’s state-run oil refiners raised gasoline and diesel prices for the first time in four years to limit their growing losses and tame domestic demand, as global crude prices remain high due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Alphabet Sells Biggest Yen Bond on Record by Foreign Issuer
item.description: Alphabet Inc. sold ¥576.5 billion ($3.6 billion) of bonds in the biggest ever yen deal by a non-Japanese company as competition to fund centers and AI infrastructure intensifies.

item.title: Yen’s Week-Long Slide Puts Traders on Guard for Intervention
item.description: Currency traders are increasingly alert to the risk of further market intervention by Japan following a 1% slide in the yen this week that has pushed it to 158 per dollar.

item.title: US Expects Dramatic Agricultural Purchases by China, Greer Says
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said he anticipates that China would commit to billions in American agricultural purchases, as Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping complete their summit in Beijing.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Denies Need for Extra Budget as Yields Rise
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama reiterated that the government doesn’t need to compile an extra budget for now, adding that a recent rise in yields on Japanese government bonds is part of a broader global trend.

item.title: Hana Buys $670 Million Stake in Crypto Exchange Operator Dunamu
item.description: Hana Bank, one of South Korea’s largest commercial lenders, has acquired a 1 trillion won ($670 million) stake in crypto exchange operator Dunamu, in the latest sign that the country’s banks are ready to embrace digital assets.

item.title: Mining Billionaire Ellison Sells Stock to Set Up Family Office
item.description: The controversial managing director of Mineral Resources Ltd., billionaire Chris Ellison, has sold shares in the company he founded as he prepares to set up his own family office.

item.title: Gautam, Sagar Adani Agree to Pay $18 Million Total Over SEC Case
item.description: Gautam Adani and his nephew Sagar agreed to pay a total of $18 million to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations they made false and misleading representations about Adani Green Energy Ltd.

item.title: Ohio Governor Candidate Ramaswamy Sells All His BuzzFeed Shares
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the biotech entrepreneur who won the Republican Party’s nomination for Ohio governor this month, has sold off all his shares in BuzzFeed Inc.

item.title: Anthropic Spat With US Emerges as Risk Factor for Figma, Others
item.description: Anthropic PBC’s legal dispute with the US government over whether the company’s artificial intelligence models will be banned from federal agencies has emerged as a financial threat to other businesses.

item.title: PE Firm Lightrock Raises $500 Million for New Clean Energy Fund
item.description: Private equity firm Lightrock, which was spun out from Liechtenstein’s LGT Group, has raised a new $500 million fund to back businesses advancing access to affordable and clean energy in Asia and Africa.

item.title: Chinese Chipmakers’ Lofty Valuations Prompt Investor Caution
item.description: A recent surge in Chinese chip stocks has made them some of the most expensive among global peers, and investors are growing increasingly cautious.

item.title: India Takes Austerity Steps to Bolster Economy as Iran War Bites
item.description: India’s government is ratcheting up steps to curb foreign outflows and cushion the economy from the fallout of the Iran war.

item.title: Fed’s Barr Says Shrinking Balance Sheet is ‘Wrong Objective’
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr on Thursday pushed back against proposals to shrink the central bank’s balance sheet, describing them as wrong and a threat to financial stability.

item.title: Gold Heads for Weekly Drop as Inflation Fuels Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold headed for a weekly decline as a war-driven surge in US inflation fuels expectations for higher interest rates.

item.title: Rally in Top Space Stocks Sets Short Sellers Up for Squeeze
item.description: Two top performing space stocks have been on blistering rallies that risk soon burning short sellers betting against the highfliers, according to data provider S3 Partners.

item.title: Apollo’s West Technology Is in Talks to Sell Remaining Business
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.-backed West Technology is engaged in exclusive talks to sell its remaining operating business to a counter-party, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Musk, Altman Make Final Pitches to Jury in Battle Over OpenAI
item.description: A jury will soon cast a vote and a judge will ultimately decide the outcome of a high-stakes showdown between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over OpenAI, the artificial intelligence startup they founded together more than a decade ago.

item.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Advance With Iran War Resolution at Impasse
item.description: Oil headed for a weekly gain as the crucial Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with efforts to end the war in limbo, prolonging disruptions that have upended global markets.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise as AI Rally Spurs Wall Street: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to rise Friday after Wall Street hit fresh records, buoyed by a rally in artificial intelligence shares, strong corporate earnings and further signs of resilience in the US consumer.
